tools.name: createConnectorProfile.postOperation
tools.description: Creates a new connector profile associated with your Amazon Web Services account. There is a soft quota of 100 connector profiles per Amazon Web Services account. If you need more connector profiles than this quota allows, you can submit a request to the Amazon AppFlow team through the Amazon AppFlow support channel. In each connector profile that you create, you can provide the credentials and properties for only one connector.

tools.name: createFlow.postOperation
tools.description: Enables your application to create a new flow using Amazon AppFlow. You must create a connector profile before calling this API. Please note that the Request Syntax below shows syntax for multiple destinations, however, you can only transfer data to one item in this list at a time. Amazon AppFlow does not currently support flows to multiple destinations at once.

tools.name: deleteConnectorProfile.postOperation
tools.description: Enables you to delete an existing connector profile.

tools.name: deleteFlow.postOperation
tools.description: Enables your application to delete an existing flow. Before deleting the flow, Amazon AppFlow validates the request by checking the flow configuration and status. You can delete flows one at a time.

tools.name: describeConnector.postOperation
tools.description: Describes the given custom connector registered in your Amazon Web Services account. This API can be used for custom connectors that are registered in your account and also for Amazon authored connectors.

tools.name: describeConnectorEntity.postOperation
tools.description: Provides details regarding the entity used with the connector, with a description of the data model for each field in that entity.

tools.name: describeConnectorProfiles.postOperation
tools.description:
Returns a list of connector-profile details matching the provided connector-profile names and connector-types. Both input lists are optional, and you can use them to filter the result.

If no names or connector-types are provided, returns all connector profiles in a paginated form. If there is no match, this operation returns an empty list.

tools.name: describeConnectors.postOperation
tools.description: Describes the connectors vended by Amazon AppFlow for specified connector types. If you don't specify a connector type, this operation describes all connectors vended by Amazon AppFlow. If there are more connectors than can be returned in one page, the response contains a nextToken object, which can be be passed in to the next call to the DescribeConnectors API operation to retrieve the next page.

tools.name: describeFlow.postOperation
tools.description: Provides a description of the specified flow.

tools.name: describeFlowExecutionRecords.postOperation
tools.description: Fetches the execution history of the flow.

tools.name: listConnectorEntities.postOperation
tools.description: Returns the list of available connector entities supported by Amazon AppFlow. For example, you can query Salesforce for Account and Opportunity entities, or query ServiceNow for the Incident entity.

tools.name: listConnectors.postOperation
tools.description: Returns the list of all registered custom connectors in your Amazon Web Services account. This API lists only custom connectors registered in this account, not the Amazon Web Services authored connectors.

tools.name: listFlows.postOperation
tools.description: Lists all of the flows associated with your account.

tools.name: registerConnector.postOperation
tools.description: Registers a new custom connector with your Amazon Web Services account. Before you can register the connector, you must deploy the associated AWS lambda function in your account.

tools.name: startFlow.postOperation
tools.description: Activates an existing flow. For on-demand flows, this operation runs the flow immediately. For schedule and event-triggered flows, this operation activates the flow.

tools.name: stopFlow.postOperation
tools.description: Deactivates the existing flow. For on-demand flows, this operation returns an unsupportedOperationException error message. For schedule and event-triggered flows, this operation deactivates the flow.

tools.name: tags.listTagsForResource
tools.description: Retrieves the tags that are associated with a specified flow.

tools.name: tags.tagResource
tools.description: Applies a tag to the specified flow.

tools.name: tags.untagResource
tools.description: Removes a tag from the specified flow.

tools.name: unregisterConnector.postOperation
tools.description: Unregisters the custom connector registered in your account that matches the connector label provided in the request.

tools.name: updateConnectorProfile.postOperation
tools.description: Updates a given connector profile associated with your account.

tools.name: updateConnectorRegistration.postOperation
tools.description:
Updates a custom connector that you've previously registered. This operation updates the connector with one of the following:

  • The latest version of the AWS Lambda function that's assigned to the connector

  • A new AWS Lambda function that you specify

tools.name: updateFlow.postOperation
tools.description: Updates an existing flow.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
